seoTitle: How to Use Headers and Footers in Google Docs (2026)
metaDescription: Add headers and footers in Google Docs, isolate your cover page from running content, and adjust margin spacing for a cleaner document layout.
intro: Headers and footers help keep titles, dates, and page metadata consistent across a long document. This guide walks through adding them via the Insert menu or a quick double-click shortcut, isolating your cover page so it stays clean, and adjusting margin spacing to match your document's design. It's a small setup step that keeps multi-page files organized.

steps.heading: Open the Headers & footers menu
steps.body: Go to Insert, then Headers & footers, to access the primary controls for adding running content to your document.

steps.heading: Use the double-click shortcut
steps.body: Double-click the top or bottom margin area of any page to instantly open the editing zone without going through the menu.

steps.heading: Add your title and typography
steps.body: Type your document title into the header and adjust font alignment so it reads clearly at the top of every page.

steps.heading: Skip the cover page
steps.body: Turn on Different first page so your header and footer content doesn't appear on your title or cover sheet.

steps.heading: Adjust margin spacing
steps.body: Open the Format menu to set custom margin dimensions and link or unlink headers across different sections as needed.

steps.heading: Combine with page numbers
steps.body: Pair your header or footer with automated page numbers to establish a consistent structural theme across long files.

faq.question: What's the fastest way to open header and footer editing?
faq.answer: Double-clicking directly on the top or bottom margin of any page opens the editing zone instantly, without navigating the Insert menu.

faq.question: Can I have different headers for different sections?
faq.answer: Yes, by inserting section breaks and unlinking headers between sections, you can run distinct content in different parts of the document.

faq.question: How do I remove the header from just my cover page?
faq.answer: Enable Different first page in the header and footer settings so the cover page displays no running content while later pages do.

faq.question: Will my header show up when I export to PDF?
faq.answer: Yes, headers and footers are part of the document layout and carry over when you export or print your file.
